scope_summary.problem: → requirements.md § R1-R5: GCP Cloud Scheduler backend not implemented, Cloud Tasks broker is a stub with no actual API calls, DelayedBroker not implemented for Cloud Tasks.
scope_summary.success_criteria: → requirements.md § R1: Cloud Scheduler implements SchedulerBackend trait. → requirements.md § R2: CloudTasksBroker publish() makes real Cloud Tasks REST API calls. → pre_clarifications Q1: use reqwest + REST API directly. → pre_clarifications Q2: full CRUD for Cloud Scheduler jobs. → pre_clarifications Q3: implement DelayedBroker with scheduleTime. → pre_clarifications Q4: separate feature flags cloudtasks + cloud-scheduler.

boundary.in_scope: → spec_plan: broker-traits (document trait hierarchy), cloudtasks-broker (complete implementation), cloud-scheduler-backend (new SchedulerBackend impl), scheduler-backends-gcp (update existing spec).
boundary.out_of_scope: Pub/Sub broker changes, NATS broker changes, Ion scheduler backend changes, worker/executor changes.
boundary.constraints: SchedulerBackend trait requires acquire_leader/renew_leader/release_leader — Cloud Scheduler backend implements these as no-ops (always returns true) since GCP manages scheduling externally. No backward compatibility concern since this is a new backend.
